name: Mincemeat Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Grease and flour three 9-inch cake pans. Set aside.
3. Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cloves, nutmeg, and cinnamon. Set aside.
4. In a large bowl, cream shortening until light and fluffy. Gradually add sugar, beating until creamy.
5. Add eggs one at a time, blending each addition.
6. Combine milk and vanilla. Stir into the creamed mixture alternately with the dry ingredients, starting and ending with the dry mix. Blend thoroughly after each addition.
7. Mix in mincemeat and chopped nuts.
8. Pour batter into prepared pans.
9. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean.
10. Remove from oven and invert pans onto cooling racks. Leave undisturbed for 10 minutes.
11. Remove cake from pans and allow to cool completely.
12. Frost with caramel frosting or your favorite frosting.
Caramel Frosting Instructions:
1. In a saucepan, combine brown sugar, butter, shortening, and salt. Heat over medium heat, stirring constantly until the mixture boils gently.
2. Gradually add milk while continuing to stir. Bring back to a boil and cook for 3 minutes.
3. Allow to cool slightly. Stir in confectioners' sugar until creamy and spreadable.
Colombo writes: "This mincemeat cake is one of our family favorites." From Alice Colombo's "Cook's Corner" column in The Courier-Journal, September 21, 1994.
